edges.description: Loss of protective BMP signaling destabilizes endothelial homeostasis.
edges.target: Pulmonary arterial endothelial dysfunction and apoptotic selection

edges.description: Reduced BMP signaling releases anti-proliferative constraints on smooth muscle cells.
edges.target: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle hyperproliferation and vasoconstrictive bias

edges.description: Endothelial dysfunction augments pathological smooth-muscle signaling.
edges.source: Pulmonary arterial endothelial dysfunction and apoptotic selection
edges.target: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle hyperproliferation and vasoconstrictive bias

edges.description: Smooth-muscle expansion contributes directly to lumen loss and resistance.
edges.source: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle hyperproliferation and vasoconstrictive bias
edges.target: Occlusive pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Metabolic stress and ROS amplify endothelial dysfunction and apoptotic selection.
edges.source: Endothelial metabolic reprogramming and mitochondrial dysfunction
edges.target: Pulmonary arterial endothelial dysfunction and apoptotic selection

edges.description: Metabolic reprogramming directly supports smooth-muscle proliferation and survival.
edges.source: Smooth muscle metabolic reprogramming and proliferative metabolism
edges.target: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle hyperproliferation and vasoconstrictive bias

edges.description: Inflammatory mediators intensify remodeling and stiffness of the pulmonary vascular wall.
edges.target: Occlusive pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

edges.description: Progressive pulmonary vascular resistance increases right-ventricular afterload.
edges.source: Occlusive pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

nodes.description: Pulmonary arterial endothelial cells undergo a Warburg-like shift toward glycolysis with impaired mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation, reducing energy availability and increasing reactive oxygen species that amplify dysfunction and apoptotic loss.
nodes.id: Endothelial metabolic reprogramming and mitochondrial dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: BMPR2 Mutation and Metabolic Reprogramming in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Metabolic abnormalities include hyperglycolytic reprogramming, mitochondrial dysfunction, aberrant polyamine and sphingosine metabolism, reduced insulin sensitivity, and defective iron handling.

nodes.description: Germline defects centered on BMPR2 and related pathway components reduce protective BMP/TGF-beta signaling in pulmonary vascular cells, creating the upstream disease trigger for HPAH.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary arterial hypertension: pathogenesis and clinical management.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the type II bone morphogenetic protein receptor (BMPR2) gene dramatically increase the risk of developing heritable PAH.

evidence.reference_title: Bone morphogenetic protein signalling in pulmonary arterial hypertension: revisiting the BMPRII connection.
evidence.snippet: BMPR2 is the most prevalent PAH gene, found in over 80% of heritable cases.

evidence.reference_title: Significance of BMPR2 mutations in pulmonary arterial hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Changes in the pulmonary vasculature caused by the disruption of BMPR2 signaling are observed in three main vascular components; endothelial cells, smooth muscle cells, and fibroblasts.

nodes.description: The combined effects of endothelial dysfunction, smooth-muscle proliferation, fibrosis, and inflammation progressively obstruct the pulmonary vascular bed and raise pulmonary vascular resistance.
nodes.id: Occlusive pulmonary vascular remodeling and increased resistance

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary arterial hypertension: pathogenesis and clinical management.
evidence.snippet: In PAH, the pulmonary vasculature is dynamically obstructed by vasoconstriction, structurally obstructed by adverse vascular remodeling, and pathologically non-compliant as a result of vascular fibrosis and stiffening.

nodes.description: Cytokine, chemokine, and immune-cell accumulation around pulmonary vessels amplifies remodeling and may be exaggerated in BMPR2-deficient vascular biology.

evidence.reference_title: Perivascular Inflammation in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: in lung biopsies from PAH patients, virtually all lineages of inflammatory cells were detected in proximity to the remodeled pulmonary vasculature

evidence.reference_title: Significance of BMPR2 mutations in pulmonary arterial hypertension.
evidence.snippet: The disruption of BMPR2 signaling pathway causes an increased degree of inflammation and decreases the ability of the immune system to resolve it.

nodes.description: Pulmonary arterial endothelial cells show early dysfunction, impaired vasodilator production, and an apoptosis-to-apoptosis-resistant transition that seeds maladaptive vascular remodeling.
nodes.id: Pulmonary arterial endothelial dysfunction and apoptotic selection

evidence.reference_title: BMPR2 Mutation and Metabolic Reprogramming in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: animal models suggests endothelial cell dysfunction

nodes.description: Pathologic signaling from dysfunctional endothelium and impaired BMP tone promotes pulmonary artery smooth muscle proliferation, narrowed vascular lumens, and increased vasoconstrictive responsiveness.
nodes.id: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle hyperproliferation and vasoconstrictive bias

biological_processes: positive regulation of smooth muscle cell proliferation

evidence.reference_title: BMPR2 Mutation and Metabolic Reprogramming in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Disease pathogenesis is driven by progressive remodeling of peripheral pulmonary arteries, caused by the excessive proliferation of vascular wall cells, including endothelial cells, smooth muscle cells and fibroblasts, and perivascular inflammation.

nodes.description: Chronic right-ventricular afterload from obstructive pulmonary vascular disease causes hypertrophy, maladaptive remodeling, and eventual right heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary arterial hypertension: pathogenesis and clinical management.
evidence.snippet: In some patients, maladaptive changes in the right ventricle, including ischemia and fibrosis, reduce right ventricular function and cause right ventricular failure.

nodes.description: Pulmonary artery smooth muscle cells undergo metabolic reprogramming with increased glycolysis and mitochondrial dysfunction that supports proliferation and apoptosis resistance, driving vascular wall expansion.
nodes.id: Smooth muscle metabolic reprogramming and proliferative metabolism

evidence.reference_title: BMPR2 Mutation and Metabolic Reprogramming in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: Metabolic abnormalities include hyperglycolytic reprogramming, mitochondrial dysfunction, aberrant polyamine and sphingosine metabolism, reduced insulin sensitivity, and defective iron handling.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary arterial hypertension: pathogenesis and clinical management.
evidence.snippet: A cancer-like increase in cell proliferation and resistance to apoptosis reflects acquired abnormalities of mitochondrial metabolism and dynamics.
